content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
July 6,2004
Hawey Gerber
7 Johnar Boulevard
Rancho Mirage, CA92270
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-04-122
Dear Mr. Gerber:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r The advice in this letter is based
solely o^n the facts provided in your letter. The Commission does not confirm telephone
advice.2
QUESTION
May you use campaign funds to make a donation to the nonprofit Mccallum
Theatre?
CONCLUSION
Yes, you may use campaign funds to make the donation because the donation
bears a reasonable relation to a govemmental purpose, provided that the McCallum
Theater is a bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt,
nonprofit organization, and no substantial part of the proceeds from the donation will
have a material financial effect on you, the campaign treasurer, or any individual with
authority to approve the expenditure of committee funds, or on a member of that person's
immediate family.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 For future reference, please also note that the Secretary of State's office has no authority to
interpret or advise regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act.


File No. A-04-122
Page No. 2
FACTS
You were re-elected to the Rancho Mirage City Council on April 13,2004. You
have approximately $30,000 in raised campaign funds which were not used during the
campaign for your re-election. You would like to donate a portion of those funds to a
nonprofit organization known as the McCallum Theatre.
The contemplated donation would be made to complete a pledge you made
personally. You have already fulfilled part of the pledge using personal funds, and you
wish to use $5,000 of the excess campaign funds to complete the pledge.
As a result of completing the pledge, your name will be placed on a donor's
plaque at the McCallum Theatre. You will not receive any financial benefit in addition to
the recognition associated with the addition of yourrurme on the donor's plaque. In a
telephone conversation June 29,2004, you clarified that you are not financially
responsible for the unpaid portion of the pledge. You also said that the McCallum
Theatre has in the past received financial support from the city and that the city uses the
theater as a venue for city-produced events.
ANALYSIS
The use of campaign funds for donations is govemed by Government Code
section 89515. That section provides:
"Campaign funds may be used to make donations or
loans to bona fide charitable, educational, civic,
religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit
organizations, where no substantial part of the
proceeds will have a material financial effect on the
candidate, elected officer, campaign treasurer, or any
individual or individuals with authority to approve the
expenditure of campaign firnds held by a committee,
or member of his or her immediate family, and where
the donation or loan bears a reasonable relation to a
political, legislativq or governmental purpose. "
In order for you to be able to donate campaign funds to the McCallum Theatre,
you must be able to answer "yes" to all three of the following questions:
l. Is the recipient organization a bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or
similar tax-exempt, nonprofi t or ganization?
2. Will no substantial part of the proceeds from the donation have a material
financial effect on you, the campaign treasurer, any individual with authority to
approve the expenditure of the funds, or any member of that person's immediate
familfl
ia


File No. A-04-122
Page No. 3
3. Does the donation bear a reasonable relation to a political, legislative, or
govefirmental purpose?
In your request for advice you identified the McCallum Theatre as a nonprofit
organization. It appears from your letter that the theatre would qualify as a "similar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization." You also indicated that you would not receive any
financial benefit and that you are not under any contractual obligation to provide the
funds. Assuming this is also true for your immediate farnily and for those with authority
to approve the expenditure of campaign funds, the remaining condition is that the
donation be reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
You may make this donation with campaign funds, provided that such a
reasonable relationship exists. The Commission has previously advised that a reasonable
relationship existed for donations to a grafifiti removal program (Neely Advice Letter, No.
4-98-133) and a local symphony (Antonovich Advice Letter, No. 4-91-476), among
others. Copies of these letters are provided for your review. You indicated that support
for and use of the theater has been apart of the city's operation and budget. AccordinglS
it appears that a donation of campaign funds to the McCallum Theatre would be an
appropriate expenditure of campaign funds reasonably related to a govemmental pu4rose.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Hal Dasinger
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosures

2 For future reference, please also note that the Secretary of State's office has no authority to
interpret or advise regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act.

QUESTION: May you use campaign funds to make a donation to the nonprofit Mccallum
Theatre?

CONCLUSION: Yes, you may use campaign funds to make the donation because the donation
bears a reasonable relation to a govemmental purpose, provided that the McCallum
Theater is a bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt,
nonprofit organization, and no substantial part of the proceeds from the donation will

immediate family.
2 For future reference, please also note that the Secretary of State's office has no authority to
interpret or advise regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act.
